pi_items.source: Grep search of 4StoreyBuilding.inp confirmed by line 2397-3176 showing SYSTEM definitions. Middle floor systems are M.S21, M.E22, M.N23, M.W24, M.C25. The values S16, E17, N18, W19, C20 refer to ground floor (G.*) plenum space identifiers at lines 1061, 1145, 1229, 1307, 1349, not middle floor systems.

Web search confirms DCV-RETURN-SENSOR is a valid option for MIN-OA-METHOD parameter in eQUEST/DOE-2. References: https://energy-models.com/training/how-model-demand-control-ventilation-dcv-equest and Energy-Models.com forum discussions on DCV implementation.

Web search confirms 1000 ppm is a standard CO2 setpoint for demand controlled ventilation, consistent with ASHRAE Standard 62-2001 Section 6.1.3. References: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8596488/ and general DCV industry standards.

Path specified in task.json metadata (line 21). Consistent with verifier.py line 33 which references RESULT_PATH = "C:\\Users\\Docker\\middle_floor_dcv_result.json". Note: Windows path, exists in Windows environment context.

Path structure is consistent with eQUEST 3.65 (as specified in env.json line 6) and Windows file system conventions. Data file exists locally at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/equest_env/data/4StoreyBuilding.inp

privileged_info_summary: The 4StoreyBuilding eQUEST model contains five middle floor packaged single-zone HVAC systems. However, the task metadata claims the systems are named M.S16, M.E17, M.N18, M.W19, and M.C20, which is INCORRECT. The actual middle floor systems in the model are M.S21, M.E22, M.N23, M.W24, and M.C25 (verified by parsing the 4StoreyBuilding.inp file). The identifiers S16, E17, N18, W19, C20 are ground floor plenum space identifiers (G.S16, G.E17, G.N18, G.W19, G.C20), not middle floor systems. The target DCV method (DCV-RETURN-SENSOR) and CO2 limit (1000 ppm) are both valid and standard for demand-controlled ventilation in eQUEST/DOE-2 modeling.
